name: 40 in Focus: SummerStage through the Lens of Photographer Jack Vartoogian
description: From 1987 to 2019, celebrated NYC photographer Jack Vartoogian captured hundreds of SummerStage performances across Manhattan parks, from Central Park to Marcus Garvey Park and Tompkins Square Park. This selection of intimate, rarely seen imagery gives viewers a look back at past decades of outdoor live music in New York City, a peek behind the curtain with never-before-seen backstage photos and honors those artists who have passed on. From the earliest performances with Sun Ra Arkestra and Mavis and Pop Staples, to legendary artists in memoriam like Tito Puente and Hugh Masekela and backstage shots of Buddy Guy and Darlene Love, this exhibition celebrates not only a photographer’s commitment to documenting performance but also celebrates a 40-year legacy of SummerStage.This free exhibition is presented by City Parks Foundation in collaboration with NYC Parks Art & Antiquities department.An opening reception will be held on June 16 from 6pm-8 pm, offering light refreshments, music and an exclusive exhibition poster while supplies last. Image credit: Sun Ra Arkestra at SummerStage, 1989, Jack Vartoogian

name: Zumba Gold®
description: Zumba Gold® offers a low-impact workout set to a fusion of Latin, International and popular music dance themes, creating a dynamic, exciting, effective fitness system! name: Summer on the Hudson: Art in the Park
description: Art in the Park invites children ages 3-8 to learn about their local environment through a mix of arts & crafts activities. This hands-on, place-based curriculum builds kids' appreciation for the plants, pollinators, and wildlife found in Riverside Park, and fosters their creativity. Mondays and Thursdays at Camel Playground; Tuesdays and Wednesdays at Hippo Playground. Art in the Park is fully funded by proceeds from the Hippo Playground Spring Fair.

name: Yoga

Experience a fun and light-hearted, mid-intensity flow that connects breath to movement as you flow through poses, with modifications for everyone. Class will focus on mobility and stability to ensure you leave feeling challenged, balanced, and relaxed. Please bring a mat! Please register for class updates. Class will be held in the Community Garden next to Gerritsen Beach Library.

name: Monday Morning Pitch In to Pick Up
description: Volunteer with us! We will be cleaning up litter to prevent it from entering the Bronx River.Your efforts not only protect wildlife & the rest of the Bronx River ecosystem, but they also help us identify the sources of trash so we can stop the pollution where it starts!

name: Trees Count at Washington Market Park
description: Volunteer with NYC Parks for Trees Count 2025! At each event you will receive a tote bag with all the materials needed to count trees. Next, we'll provide a 30-minute training session that includes the basics of tree identification and how to measure and assess each tree. Using our Tree Census app, you’ll split up into different zones within the park to start counting trees! After completing your first training event, you are welcome to continue counting trees on your own in smaller parks across the city. For your comfort and safety at the event please bring water and wear close-toed shoes.

name: Reel Talks at Bryant Park: Conversation on Eric Rohmer
description: Enjoy a conversation on film director Eric Rohmer with André Aciman, hosted by film connoisseur Scott Adlerberg.André Aciman is the New York Times bestselling author of Call Me by Your Name, Out of Egypt, Eight White Nights, False Papers, Alibis, Harvard Square, Enigma Variations, Homo Irrealis, Roman Year, and Find Me. His most recent book is Room on the Sea (2025). He’s the editor of The Proust Project and teaches comparative literature at the Graduate Center of the City University of New York. He lives with his wife in Manhattan.Scott Adlerberg is the author of five novels, including Graveyard Love, Jack Waters, and The Screaming Child. He has written many short stories and contributes pieces regularly to sites such as CrimeReads and Mystery Tribune. He was born in New York City and lives in Brooklyn.

name: Bryant Park Movie Nights
description: Movie Nights at Bryant Park returns for its 33rd season! Bring a blanket and a friend and grab your spot on the lawn to watch a feature film on the big screen under the stars. The lawn opens at 5pm and films begin at 8pm.Food items for sale curated by Hester Street Fair are available 4pm-8:30pm on the Fountain Terrace, and beer and wine can be purchased on the western end of the lawn beginning at 5pm. Paramount+ Movie Nights at Bryant Park are supported by Time Out and Sour Patch Kids.Jul 13: Wayne's WorldJul 20: Good Morning VietnamJul 27: The Truman ShowAug 03: Bridget Jones's DiaryAug 10: Catch Me If You CanAug 17: City of GodAug 24: King CreoleAug 31: No Country for Old MenSep 07: Galaxy QuestSept 14: Shakespeare in Love

No registration is required, just stop by and join us!

With a list of past featured artists including Lana Del Ray, Anais Mitchel and Lucius, it may even be an opportunity to see the next big star in action.

description: Uplift your spirit with this dance fitness class! Come and enjoy an array of upbeat music featuring gospel. This class is a fusion of motivation, inspiration, fitness, and fun. Move your body, relax your mind, enjoy the community, and lift your spirit!

All movements are easy on the joints while enhancing flexibility, posture, and overall well-being. Ideal for beginners, older adults, or anyone looking for a supportive way to stay active.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Exercise Classes,Yoga & Pilates Classes

name: BollyX
description: BollyX is a Bollywood inspired cardio dance fitness class inspired and is driven by the heart-pumping, energetic rhythms of cultural music from all parts of India. The class embodies dynamic choreography and easy-to-follow dance aerobics. Choreography and workout routines have been set so everyone can enjoy the class (modifications for individual steps can be provided in class). No dance experience or Bollywood familiarity is required - just come with comfortable shoes, water, and your smiles!Participants must sign in at the front desk and bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Dance Classes,Exercise Classes

name: RETRO Dance Fitness
description: Come dance to classic pop tunes: Top 40, Disco, Electronic and more! This class is 45 minutes of peppy beats to enhance your cardio conditioning. A low-impact and fun workout; it is open to any fitness level. Let's dance away blue Monday!

Paces available include 7 min to 12 min, run/walk, and walk groups. Open to all ages and abilities. Bag drop available, though not guaranteed.

Here, we focus on functional, everyday movement with the goal of increasing your overall level of fitness. You can expect several rounds of timed exercises including strength and cardio followed by periods of rest. Sets and rounds vary class to class. All exercises will be demonstrated and cued for participants. Modifications given as needed.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Bodyweight Power Circuit
description: A full-body circuit designed to build strength, power, endurance, and confidence using just your bodyweight. Expect athletic movement, scalable progressions, and high-energy intervals that challenge every fitness level.

secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Virtual Yoga
description: Welcome to this online yoga community in Vinyasa Yoga Flow with Rika. This class encompasses a warm-up, mindful flow, balance poses, gentle inversions, and final relaxation. Discover empowerment and affirmation to embrace who you are on and off your mat. Mats and props are recommended but not required. Leave feeling refreshed and centered, and knowing you have a safe community. The Zoom link will be sent out two hours before the class and again right before the class starts to registered participants.
